content.problemStatement:
A **mobile** is a rooted tree with weighted leaves that satisfies the following property: each non-leaf vertex vvv has exactly two children L(v)L(v)L(v) and R(v)R(v)R(v), and the total weight of leaves in the subtree of L(v)L(v)L(v) is equal to the total weight of leaves in the subtree of R(v)R(v)R(v).

We have nnn positive integer weights a1,‚Ä¶,ana_1, \ldots, a_na1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,an‚Äã. You have to count the number of **suitable** mobiles. A mobile is **suitable** if the multiset of weights in its leaves is equal to the multiset {a1,‚Ä¶,an}\{a_1, \ldots, a_n\}{a1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,an‚Äã}. Two mobiles are considered different if they are not **equal**. Two mobiles T1T_1T1‚Äã and T2T_2T2‚Äã are **equal** in one of two cases:


T1T_1T1‚Äã and T2T_2T2‚Äã each consist of a single leaf vertex, and the leaves' weights are equal;
Respective roots r1r_1r1‚Äã and r2r_2r2‚Äã of T1T_1T1‚Äã and T2T_2T2‚Äã are both not leaves, and subtrees of L(r1)L(r_1)L(r1‚Äã) and L(r2)L(r_2)L(r2‚Äã) are equal mobiles, as well as subtrees of R(r1)R(r_1)R(r1‚Äã) and R(r2)R(r_2)R(r2‚Äã).
Since the answer can be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. TTT test case descriptions follow.
Each test case description starts with a line containing a single integer nnn, the number of weights. The next line contains nnn integers a1,‚Ä¶,ana_1, \ldots, a_na1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,an‚Äã .


Output Format
Print answers for each test case in a new line, in the order of appearance in the input. For each test case print a single integer, the number of different suitable mobiles modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of all nnn does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105.
